title: valeriana sitchensis subsp. scouleri
intro: Valeriana sitchensis subsp. scouleri, also known as Scouler's valerian, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the valerian family, native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including sedative and antispasmodic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of valerenic acid and other compounds. The health benefits of Scouler's valerian include relief from insomnia, anxiety, and restlessness, as well as its ability to soothe muscle and nerve pain. Valeriana sitchensis subsp. scouleri is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is often recommended by herbalists for its calming and sleep-promoting effects.
